findings.title: Human changed-path proof is not isolated from preflight output
findings.rationale: The new non-JSON test asserts that stdout contains "changed paths:" and the changed path strings, but `codex1 setup` prints both a preflight list and the final human report to stdout. Because the preflight output uses the same path formatting, the test can pass even if the final `changed paths` section regresses or omits entries. That means the repaired proof still overclaims the specific human-output behavior it is supposed to prove.
findings.suggested_next_action: Tighten the test to isolate the final human report section, for example by splitting on the `changed paths:` header or by asserting the exact rendered report block, so the proof cannot be satisfied by preflight output alone.
